2606.07356.title: DirectAudioEdit: Inversion-Free Text-Guided Audio Editing via Diffusion Prediction Contrast
2606.07356.paper_summary: Text-guided audio editing aims to modify the language-specified acoustic content while preserving edit-irrelevant source components. Existing training-free methods typically rely on inversion-based editing. While inversion-free editing is appealing as it decreases computational overhead and reconstruction errors, it remains largely unexplored for audio editing. The key challenge is to construct a source-to-target editing path through diffusion denoising dynamics. In this paper, we introduce DirectAudioEdit, the first attempt to develop a training-free and inversion-free method for audio editing. Experiments on music and event-level benchmarks across two backbones show that DirectAudioEdit reduces macro-averaged FAD and KL by 15.9% and 15.8% compared with DDPM inversion, while achieving up to 64.5% editing speedup.
2606.07356.paper_summary_zh: 文本引導的音頻編輯旨在修改語言指定的聲學內容，同時保留與編輯無關的源組件。現有的無需訓練的方法通常依賴於基於反演的編輯。雖然無反演編輯因其減少計算負擔和重建誤差而具有吸引力，但在音頻編輯中仍然大多未被探索。關鍵挑戰是通過擴散去噪動力學構建源到目標的編輯路徑。在本文中，我們介紹了DirectAudioEdit，這是首次嘗試開發一種無需訓練且無反演的方法來進行音頻編輯。在兩個基礎模型上的音樂和事件級基準實驗表明，與DDPM反演相比，DirectAudioEdit將宏觀平均FAD和KL分別減少了15.9%和15.8%，同時實現了高達64.5%的編輯加速。
2606.07356.author: Zhengkun Ge et.al.
2606.07356.authors: Zhengkun Ge, Xiaoqian Liu, Haoran Zhang, Yuan Ge, Junxiang Zhang, Zhengtao Yu, Jingbo Zhu, Tong Xiao
